---
title: "Prescriptive Assignment 3 Report"
author: "Rick de Rijk, Tycho van Rooij, Stefan Vonk & Garoe"
subtitle: "Prediction & Predictive coming together"
format:
  pdf:
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        \usepackage{enumitem}
        \setlist[itemize]{itemsep=2pt, topsep=2pt, parsep=0pt, partopsep=0pt}
        \setlist[enumerate]{itemsep=2pt, topsep=2pt, parsep=0pt, partopsep=0pt}
        \usepackage{setspace}
        \setstretch{0.96}
        \usepackage{parskip}
        \setlength{\parskip}{0.3em}
        \setlength{\parindent}{0pt}
geometry:
      - margin=2cm
editor: visual
toc: true
lof: true
number-sections: true
---

\newpage

# Introduction

-   Brief description of the maintenance scheduling problem\
-   Explanation of Remaining Useful Lifetime (RUL)\
-   Overview of the two tasks: Prediction and Optimization\
-   Structure of the report

# Prediction Task

## Data Analysis and Feature Engineering

-   Description of dataset 1\
-   Explanation of how RUL is constructed from the data\
-   Exploratory data analysis\
-   Feature engineering steps\
-   Final feature selection

## Model Development

-   Model choice: XGBoost\
-   Data splitting strategy\
-   Hyperparameter choices\
-   Validation approach\
-   Evaluation metric and justification

## Prediction Results

-   Predicted RUL values for dataset 2\
-   Rounding to integer values\
-   Summary of predictions

## Comparison with Consultancy Predictions

-   Description of consultancy dataset\
-   Comparison method\
-   Results of comparison\
-   Interpretation and discussion

# Optimization Task

## Problem Formulation

-   Description of maintenance scheduling problem\
-   Constraints\
-   Objective: minimize penalty costs

## Genetic Algorithm Design

### Representation

-   Description of an individual

### Selection Strategy

-   Method used

### Crossover

-   Description of crossover method

### Mutation

-   Description of mutation method or justification

### Feasibility Handling

-   How infeasible solutions are handled

### Hyperparameters

-   Population size\
-   Crossover probability\
-   Mutation probability\
-   Other design choices

## Optimization Results

-   Best solution found\
-   Description of maintenance schedule\
-   Total penalty cost

## Performance Analysis

-   Description of 30 runs experiment\
-   Average-best-fitness plot\
-   Interpretation of results

## Comparison with Consultancy RUL

-   Results using consultancy predictions\
-   Comparison with own predictions\
-   Explanation of differences

# Discussion

-   Key insights\
-   Strengths of the approach\
-   Limitations

# Conclusion

-   Summary of findings\
-   Final remarks

# Appendix

-   Additional figures\
-   Extra explanations

# References

-   Sources used
